description: The ancestral group or ethnicity of the participants. Keywords: Ancestry, Race, Ethnic.
examples: European (EUR), African American (AA), East Asian (EAS), Caribbean Hispanic

description: Study phase indicating whether results come from discovery, replication, meta-analysis, or combined analysis. Keywords: Stage, Phase, Discovery, Replication, Meta, Combined.
examples: Discovery, Replication, Stage 1, Stage 2, Meta-analysis, Joint analysis

description: The reference panel or method used to infer unobserved genotypes. Keywords: Imputation, Reference panel, Phasing.
examples: 1000 Genomes (1000G), Haplotype Reference Consortium (HRC), TOPMed, UK10K

description: Type of genetic data or study design used to generate the results. Keywords: Study type, Genotyping, Sequencing.
examples: GWAS, Whole Exome Sequencing (WES), Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS), Array genotyping

description: The specific trait, disease, or outcome being analyzed. Keywords: Trait, Disease, Outcome.
examples: Alzheimer's disease, BMI, LDL cholesterol, Type 2 diabetes, Blood pressure

description: Details of the model that produces the association record. 1) is that a meta-analyses or joint analyses; 2) what kind of regression; 3) what are the covariates; 4) p-value correction method. i.e Inverse-variance weighted Meta-analysis; logistic regression; adjusting for age, sex, first 3 pcs; p value bonferroni correction; other.... Notes: to determine whether it is a meta-analysis, search Meta, Meta-analysis, METAL, inverse-variance weighted, fixed effect
Type of analysis: Age at onset (AAO) / Survival, Cognitive, Cross phenotype, Disease risk, Endophenotype, eQTL, Neuropathology, Pleiotropy
The specific study or database name. Keywords: Study, Dataset, Discovery.
examples: ADNI, IGAP, UK Biobank, ADGC, CHARGE, EADI
